results.definition: a group or collection of things that belong together
examples: She bought a complete set of encyclopedias.
He owns a full set of vintage trading cards.

results.definition: to put, lay, or stand something in a specified place or position
examples: She set the vase on the windowsill.
He set his bag down and took a deep breath.

results.definition: (of the sun or moon) to go below the horizon
examples: The sun set behind the mountains in a blaze of orange.
They watched the moon set over the ocean.

results.definition: fixed or established in advance; not changing
examples: The meeting starts at a set time every Monday.
She followed a set routine every morning without fail.

results.definition: the scenery and props used in a theatrical or film production
examples: The set was designed to look like a 1920s New York apartment.
The entire cast gathered on set for the final scene.

results.definition: existing or available in large quantities; more than enough
examples: The region has abundant natural resources.
There was abundant evidence to support the claim.

results.definition: well meaning and kindly disposed toward others
examples: She was a benevolent leader who always looked after her people.
The benevolent donor gave millions to the hospital.

results.definition: giving a lot of information clearly and in few words; brief but comprehensive
examples: His concise explanation made the topic easy to understand.
Please write a concise summary of the report.

results.definition: having or showing care and conscientiousness in one's work or duties
examples: She was a diligent student who never missed a deadline.
His diligent efforts finally paid off.

results.definition: fluent or persuasive in speaking or writing
examples: She gave an eloquent speech that moved the entire audience.
He was an eloquent writer whose prose flowed beautifully.

results.definition: to grow or develop in a healthy or vigorous way
examples: The business began to flourish after the new manager took over.
Plants flourish in warm, humid conditions.

results.definition: a bold or extravagant gesture or action made especially to attract attention
examples: He signed his name with a dramatic flourish.
She entered the room with a flourish.

results.definition: the quality of being thankful and appreciative
examples: She expressed her gratitude with a heartfelt letter.
He felt deep gratitude toward those who had helped him.

results.definition: the quality of forming a pleasing and consistent whole
examples: The colors in the painting were in perfect harmony.
The two nations lived in harmony for decades.

results.definition: the combination of simultaneously sounded musical notes to produce a pleasing effect
examples: The choir sang in beautiful three-part harmony.

results.definition: to light up or brighten a place
examples: Candles illuminated the dark room.
The streetlamps illuminate the road at night.

results.definition: to help clarify or explain something
examples: The documentary illuminated the struggles of rural communities.
His lecture illuminated a complex scientific concept.

results.definition: feeling or expressing great happiness and triumph
examples: The team was jubilant after winning the championship.
A jubilant crowd filled the streets in celebration.

results.definition: having or showing eagerness or enthusiasm
examples: She was keen to learn everything about photography.
He showed a keen interest in the project.

results.definition: having a sharp edge or point
examples: The chef used a keen blade to slice the vegetables.

results.definition: to mourn or express deep sorrow or regret
examples: She lamented the loss of her childhood home.
He lamented that he had never learned to play the piano.

results.definition: a passionate expression of grief or sorrow
examples: The poem was a lament for a lost era.
His song was a lament for those who had fallen in battle.

results.definition: showing great attention to detail or being very careful and precise
examples: The architect was meticulous in reviewing every drawing.
Her meticulous approach ensured the project had no errors.

results.definition: a spoken or written account of connected events; a story
examples: The novel's narrative spans three generations.
She told a compelling narrative about her travels.

results.definition: relating to or characteristic of narration
examples: The film uses a narrative structure that jumps back and forth in time.

results.definition: no longer produced or used; out of date
examples: Fax machines have become largely obsolete.
Many obsolete words have disappeared from everyday speech.

results.definition: to continue in a course of action despite difficulty or delay in achieving success
examples: Despite the setbacks, she persevered and finished her degree.
You must persevere if you want to master a new skill.

results.definition: attractively unusual or old-fashioned in character
examples: The village was full of quaint little cottages.
She had a quaint way of expressing herself.

results.definition: able to withstand or recover quickly from difficult conditions
examples: Children are often more resilient than adults expect.
The resilient community rebuilt quickly after the flood.

results.definition: calm, peaceful, and untroubled; tranquil
examples: She remained serene even in the face of chaos.
The lake looked serene in the early morning light.

results.definition: holding firmly to something; very determined and persistent
examples: She was tenacious in her pursuit of justice.
His tenacious grip on the rope saved his life.

results.definition: being the only one of its kind; unlike anything else
examples: Each snowflake has a unique pattern.
Her singing voice was truly unique.

results.definition: producing powerful feelings or strong, clear images in the mind
examples: She had a vivid memory of the accident.
The artist used vivid colors to capture the sunset.

results.definition: feeling or showing caution about possible dangers or problems
examples: She was wary of strangers after the incident.
Investors were wary of putting money into the volatile market.

results.definition: to have an intense feeling of longing for something, typically something lost or out of reach
examples: She yearned to return to her hometown.
He yearned for a simpler way of life.

results.definition: great energy or enthusiasm in pursuit of a cause or objective
examples: She approached every task with incredible zeal.
His zeal for reform made him a powerful political figure.

results.definition: open to more than one interpretation; not having one obvious meaning
examples: The instructions were ambiguous and caused a lot of confusion.
His ambiguous response left everyone guessing.

results.definition: concise and exact use of words in writing or speech
examples: The essay was praised for its clarity and brevity.
Brevity is the soul of wit, as Shakespeare wrote.

results.definition: truthful and straightforward; frank and honest
examples: She gave a candid account of what had happened.
I appreciated his candid feedback on my work.

results.definition: the ability to understand and share the feelings of another person
examples: Good doctors show empathy toward their patients.
She listened with empathy as he described his struggles.

results.definition: sparing or economical with regard to money or food; avoiding waste
examples: She was frugal with her savings and rarely splurged.
His frugal lifestyle allowed him to retire early.

results.definition: a large, heavy mammal with thick fur and a short tail
examples: A bear wandered into the campsite.
The bear hibernates during winter.

results.definition: to carry or support the weight of something
examples: The bridge can bear the weight of heavy trucks.
She bore the responsibility with grace.

results.definition: to endure or tolerate something difficult
examples: I can't bear the sound of nails on a chalkboard.
He bore the pain without complaint.

results.definition: an investor who expects prices to fall in the financial market
examples: The bears on Wall Street predicted a market crash.
He was a bear on tech stocks.

results.definition: to throw something forcefully in a specified direction
examples: He cast the fishing line into the river.
She cast the stone across the water.

results.definition: the actors taking part in a play, film, or other production
examples: The cast of the film gave outstanding performances.
The entire cast took a bow at the end.

results.definition: to assign a role to an actor in a production
examples: She was cast as the villain in the new thriller.
The director cast unknown actors in the lead roles.

results.definition: a hard protective shell worn around a broken limb while it heals
examples: He had to wear a cast on his arm for six weeks.
Everyone signed their name on her cast.

results.definition: a preliminary version of a piece of writing
examples: She submitted the first draft of her novel to the editor.
This is just a rough draft; it still needs a lot of work.

results.definition: a current of cool air in a room or enclosed space
examples: There was a cold draft coming through the window.
Close the door — there's a draft in here.

results.definition: to write a preliminary version of a document
examples: The lawyer drafted the contract overnight.
He drafted a letter of complaint to the company.

results.definition: compulsory recruitment into military service
examples: Many young men tried to avoid the draft during the war.
The government reinstated the draft during the national emergency.

results.definition: to occupy or attract someone's interest or attention
examples: The speaker struggled to engage the audience.
A good book engages your imagination.

results.definition: to participate or become involved in something
examples: Students are encouraged to engage in extracurricular activities.
He refused to engage in the debate.

results.definition: to hire or employ someone
examples: The firm engaged a consultant to review their processes.
They engaged a new lawyer for the case.

results.definition: to begin fighting or come into conflict with an enemy
examples: The troops engaged the enemy at dawn.
The two fleets engaged in a fierce naval battle.

results.definition: treating people equally without favoritism or discrimination
examples: The judge was known for being fair and impartial.
All employees deserve fair treatment.

results.definition: (of weather) fine and dry
examples: We were lucky to have fair weather for the picnic.
The forecast promises fair skies all weekend.

results.definition: a public event with rides, games, and entertainment
examples: The children were excited to go to the county fair.
She won a goldfish at the fair.

results.definition: having light-colored hair or skin
examples: She had fair hair and blue eyes.
People with fair skin should wear sunscreen.

results.definition: to seize and hold something firmly
examples: She grasped the railing to stop herself from falling.
He grasped the rope with both hands.

results.definition: to understand something fully
examples: It took a while to grasp the complexity of the problem.
She grasped the concept immediately.

results.definition: a firm hold or grip on something
examples: The wet bar slipped from his grasp.
She kept the document firmly within her grasp.

results.definition: a person's understanding or knowledge of something
examples: He has an excellent grasp of mathematics.
The topic is within every student's grasp.

results.definition: a place on the coast where ships may moor in shelter
examples: The fishing boats returned to the harbor at dusk.
The storm made it impossible to leave the harbor.

results.definition: to keep a thought or feeling, especially a negative one, in one's mind
examples: She harbored a deep resentment toward her former boss.
He harbored doubts about the plan for years.

results.definition: to give shelter or refuge to someone, especially a criminal
examples: He was accused of harboring a fugitive.
The family harbored refugees during the conflict.

results.definition: to deduce or conclude information from evidence and reasoning
examples: From his silence, she inferred that he disagreed.
We can infer from the data that sales are declining.

results.definition: to suggest or imply something indirectly
examples: Are you inferring that I made a mistake?
His tone inferred disapproval without saying it outright.

results.definition: to point to something as a logical consequence
examples: The evidence infers a pattern of deliberate misconduct.
This result infers the need for further testing.

results.definition: a hard, typically green stone used for ornaments and jewelry
examples: She wore a necklace carved from jade.
The museum displayed ancient jade artifacts.

results.definition: a shade of green resembling the color of jade stone
examples: The walls were painted a soothing jade.
Her eyes were a deep shade of jade.

results.definition: to make someone feel tired, bored, or lacking enthusiasm through overexposure
examples: Years of travel had jaded her sense of wonder.
The repetitive work jaded even the most enthusiastic employees.

results.definition: to light or set fire to something
examples: He kindled a fire to keep warm through the night.
She kindled the campfire with dry twigs.

results.definition: to arouse or inspire a feeling or emotion
examples: The trip kindled a passion for art in her.
His words kindled hope in the hearts of the people.

results.definition: a group of kittens born to the same mother at one time
examples: The cat gave birth to a kindle of five kittens.
The kindle was found huddled together in the barn.

results.definition: to be in or move into a sloping position
examples: She leaned against the wall and waited.
The old tower leans slightly to one side.

results.definition: thin, especially healthily so; having little fat
examples: Athletes tend to have lean, muscular bodies.
He looked lean and fit after months of training.

results.definition: (of meat) containing little fat
examples: Choose lean cuts of meat for a healthier diet.
The recipe calls for lean ground beef.

results.definition: to rely on or turn to someone for support
examples: She leaned on her friends during the difficult period.
He leaned on his mentor for advice throughout his career.

results.definition: clear and obvious to the eye or mind
examples: Her disappointment was manifest in her expression.
There was a manifest lack of preparation for the event.

results.definition: to display or show a quality or feeling by one's acts
examples: The illness can manifest as fatigue and fever.
His anxiety manifested itself in nervous laughter.

results.definition: a document listing the cargo, passengers, or contents of a ship or aircraft
examples: The customs officer checked the ship's manifest.
All goods must be declared on the cargo manifest.

results.definition: a long fictional prose narrative
examples: She read the novel in a single weekend.
His debut novel won several prestigious awards.

results.definition: interestingly new or unusual; not previously encountered
examples: Scientists developed a novel approach to treating the disease.
The startup had a novel idea that disrupted the industry.

results.definition: relating to or characteristic of a novel as a literary form
examples: The novel form allows deep exploration of character.
He studied novel structure in his creative writing course.

results.definition: not discovered or known about; uncertain
examples: He was an obscure poet until his work was rediscovered.
The film was based on an obscure medieval legend.

results.definition: not clearly expressed or easily understood
examples: The instructions were too obscure to follow.
His writing style is deliberately obscure.

results.definition: to keep from being seen; to conceal or make unclear
examples: Fog obscured the view of the mountains.
His smile obscured his true feelings.

results.definition: not decorated or elaborate; simple in character
examples: She wore a plain white dress to the ceremony.
The room was furnished in a plain, minimalist style.

results.definition: easy to understand or perceive; clear
examples: The instructions were written in plain English.
It was plain that she was upset.

results.definition: a large area of flat land with few trees
examples: Herds of buffalo once roamed the great plains.
The plain stretched as far as the eye could see.

results.definition: not attractive or good-looking
examples: He described himself as plain but charming.
She had a plain appearance but a magnetic personality.

results.definition: the period during which a monarch rules
examples: The queen's reign lasted over seventy years.
During the reign of King Henry VIII, England changed dramatically.

results.definition: to hold royal office; to rule as a king or queen
examples: She reigned over the kingdom for four decades.
The emperor reigned but did not govern directly.

results.definition: to be the best or most powerful in a particular area
examples: Silence reigned in the courtroom after the verdict.
Chaos reigned in the streets following the announcement.

results.definition: rising or falling sharply; nearly perpendicular
examples: The hikers struggled up the steep mountain path.
The road had a steep incline that tested the engine.

results.definition: (of a price) very high and possibly unreasonable
examples: The hotel rates were a bit steep for a budget traveler.
The fines for parking violations are steep in this city.

results.definition: to soak something in liquid in order to soften or flavor it
examples: Steep the tea leaves in hot water for five minutes.
The fruit was steeped in brandy overnight.

results.definition: to pervade or fill with a quality or influence
examples: The city is steeped in history and tradition.
She steeped herself in the culture of the country she visited.

results.definition: a person's state of mind or mood, especially regarding calmness or anger
examples: He had a short temper and would snap easily.
She managed to keep her temper throughout the argument.

results.definition: to make something less extreme or intense; to moderate
examples: She tempered her criticism with some genuine praise.
The warm weather tempered the harshness of winter.

results.definition: to harden metal by heating and then cooling it
examples: The blacksmith tempered the steel blade in cold water.
Properly tempered metal is far more resilient.

results.definition: to tune a piano or other instrument to a standard temperament
examples: The technician came to temper the grand piano before the concert.
A well-tempered instrument sounds harmonious across all keys.

results.definition: a risky or daring journey or undertaking
examples: Starting a business is always a risky venture.
Their joint venture brought together two rival companies.

results.definition: to dare to do something or go somewhere that may be dangerous
examples: She ventured into the dark forest alone.
He ventured an opinion despite the hostile crowd.

results.definition: to put something at risk in the hope of a greater gain
examples: He ventured his entire savings on the new startup.
Nothing ventured, nothing gained.

results.definition: to hold and use a weapon or tool effectively
examples: The knight wielded his sword with great skill.
She wielded the hammer and drove the nail in one blow.

results.definition: to have and use power or influence
examples: The CEO wielded enormous influence over the board.
She wielded her authority with fairness and conviction.

results.definition: to manage or control something with skill
examples: He wielded language with the precision of a poet.
She wielded complex data to make her argument compelling.
